name: Defensive Bastion
calculationsAnalysis: The Defensive Bastion represents major military infrastructure with modest revenue. The construction cost of 14,000,000 ducats reflects the massive fortifications and artillery platforms required, while generating 20,000 ducats income reflects security fees from protected areas and increased property values in secured districts. The maintenance cost of 75,000 ducats creates a substantial ongoing expense partially offset by this income. The employment capacity of 40 represents the soldiers, gunners, and support staff needed. The construction time of 30 days (2592000000ms) reflects the massive defensive project. This building creates a strategic military asset with appropriate government restrictions reflecting the historical importance of artillery fortifications in Venice's defensive strategy, where control of key approaches was essential to the city's security given its vulnerable lagoon position.
shortDescription: Fortified defensive position with artillery platforms protecting strategic approaches.
fullDescription: Protecting Venice's vulnerable approaches, defensive bastions provided artillery positions and observation posts at strategic locations. Featuring angled walls designed for cannon placement, ammunition storage, garrison quarters, and often signal stations, these substantial fortifications represented Renaissance military engineering. Venice's unique lagoon position required specialized defensive structures rather than traditional city walls.
flavorText: While other cities hide behind walls, Venice stands protected by water and resolve—yet where those natural defenses might be challenged, we ensure our cannons speak with decisive authority.

locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Commanding position, field of fire, supply access
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Creates military presence, security enhancement, strategic control

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian defensive bastion under construction, substantial military fortification, massive earthworks and foundation creating angled walls optimized for artillery, cannon platforms taking shape with geometric precision for maximum firing arcs, ammunition magazines with safety features, garrison quarters, command post with strategic visibility, reinforced construction designed to withstand bombardment, supply access routes, strategic positioning showing defensive mindset, military engineers supervising precise angles and measurements, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on Renaissance military engineering principles applied to Venice's unique defensive needs
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian defensive bastion, imposing military fortification featuring geometric angled walls designed to deflect incoming fire while supporting artillery, multiple cannon positions on elevated platforms with optimal firing arcs over approaches, ammunition magazines built into structure with safety features, garrison quarters for permanent defensive force, command post with maps and firing tables, signal station for communication with other defenses, Venetian military standards flying, artillery crews maintaining cannons, officers observing approaches, thick walls capable of absorbing bombardment, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, sense of formidable defensive position, mathematical precision of Renaissance military engineering, strategic positioning demonstrating defensive control of vital approach, combination of defensive architecture and offensive firepower, soldiers conducting drills, gunners calculating range to targets

upgradePath: Advanced Star Fort (improved artillery placement, enhanced defense)
